citation: BlueCare PPO §4.2.1 — Advanced Imaging of the Knee
fullText: MRI of the knee without contrast (CPT 73721) requires at least 6 weeks of documented conservative treatment including physical therapy, NSAIDs, bracing, or activity modification AND a diagnosis consistent with osteoarthritis (M17.x), ligamentous injury (S83.x), or meniscal derangement (M23.x).

citation: BlueCare PPO §4.2.2 — Knee MRI with Contrast
fullText: MRI of the knee with contrast (CPT 73722) follows the same clinical prerequisites as 73721 and additionally requires documentation that contrast enhancement will materially affect diagnostic yield.

citation: BlueCare PPO §4.2.3 — Knee MRI with and without Contrast
fullText: MRI of the knee with and without contrast (CPT 73723) is reserved for complex joint pathology requiring pre- and post-contrast comparison.
MRI of the shoulder requires 6 weeks of conservative treatment and a diagnosis of rotator cuff pathology (M75.x), sprain (S43.x), or chronic shoulder pain (M25.51/52).

MRI of the lumbar spine requires 6 weeks of conservative treatment and a diagnosis of disc disorder (M51.x), radiculopathy (M54.16), or nerve root compression (G55). Office (POS 11) or outpatient hospital (POS 22) settings only.

citation: BlueCare PPO §4.3.3 — Lumbar MRI with Contrast
fullText: MRI of the lumbar spine with contrast is limited to patients with prior non-contrast study showing indeterminate findings, or suspected infection/neoplasm.
MRI of the cervical spine requires 4 weeks of conservative treatment and a cervical disc (M50.x) or radiculopathy (M54.x) diagnosis, or documented trauma.

citation: BlueCare PPO §4.1.2 — Brain MRI without Contrast
fullText: Brain MRI without contrast is indicated for persistent headache with red-flag symptoms, focal neurologic deficit, suspected stroke, or lesion screening.

citation: BlueCare PPO §4.1.1 — Brain MRI w/ and w/o Contrast
fullText: Brain MRI with and without contrast (CPT 70553) is indicated for suspected intracranial mass, demyelinating disease, or infectious process.
CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast (CPT 74177) is indicated for acute abdomen, suspected inflammatory bowel disease, mass workup, or biliary pathology.
CT chest with contrast is indicated for acute chest pain workup with positive D-dimer, suspected pulmonary embolism, or lung mass evaluation.
Knee arthroscopy with meniscectomy (CPT 29881) requires 8 weeks of failed conservative treatment and documented meniscal pathology on MRI.
TKA (CPT 27447) requires 12 weeks of failed conservative tx, documented osteoarthritis (M17.x), and radiographic evidence of joint-space narrowing.
Lumbar laminectomy/discectomy (CPT 63030) requires 12 weeks of failed conservative tx AND confirmed disc pathology or radiculopathy on MRI.
Cardiac catheterization with coronary angiography is indicated for suspected coronary artery disease with positive stress test or unstable symptoms.
